description: Skilled Nominated (190) and Skilled Work Regional (491) seed data. These subclasses share the same core evidence and requirements as the Skilled Independent (189), with the addition of a state/territory or regional nomination requirement.

differences_from_189.190: Requires a valid nomination from an Australian state or territory government.
differences_from_189.491: Requires a valid nomination from an Australian state/territory government OR sponsorship by an eligible relative living in a designated regional area.

additional_evidence_items.title: State or territory government nomination
additional_evidence_items.description: A valid nomination from an Australian state or territory government for the nominated occupation.
examples: Nomination outcome letter from state/territory government
Confirmatory email from state/territory skills migration program

additional_requirements.title: State or territory nomination
additional_requirements.description: The applicant must hold a valid nomination from an Australian state or territory government.

additional_flags.title: State or territory nomination not confirmed
additional_flags.why_it_matters: The Subclass 190 visa requires a valid state/territory nomination. Without it, the application cannot proceed regardless of points score.
actions: Confirm your nomination application has been submitted and approved
Ensure your occupation and skills assessment match the state's requirements at time of nomination
evidence_examples: Nomination outcome letter from state migration program
Skills assessment matching the nominated occupation

additional_flags.title: Points claimed may exceed what evidence supports
additional_flags.why_it_matters: Like the 189, the 190 uses a points test. Nominated applicants receive 5 bonus points from nomination, but all other claimed points must still be evidenced.
actions: Recalculate points including the 5-point nomination bonus
Verify each point claim against supporting documents
evidence_examples: Employment reference letters covering claimed experience period
English test result matching bandwidth claimed

additional_evidence_items.title: State/territory nomination OR eligible relative sponsorship
additional_evidence_items.description: Either a valid nomination from an Australian state or territory government, or sponsorship by an eligible relative living and working in a designated regional area.
examples: Nomination outcome letter from state/territory government
Evidence that relative lives in a designated regional area

additional_requirements.title: State nomination or eligible relative sponsorship
additional_requirements.description: The applicant must hold a valid state/territory nomination or sponsorship by an eligible relative in a regional area.

additional_requirements.title: Live and work in designated regional area
additional_requirements.description: 491 holders must live and work (or study) in a designated regional area of Australia for at least 3 years before being eligible for the permanent pathway (191).

additional_flags.title: Nomination or relative sponsorship not confirmed
additional_flags.why_it_matters: The 491 requires either state/territory nomination or sponsorship by an eligible relative in a regional area. Without one of these, the application cannot proceed.
actions: Confirm whether you are applying via state nomination or relative sponsorship
Upload the nomination or sponsorship approval letter
Ensure your relative's regional address is documented if applying via sponsorship
evidence_examples: Proof of relative's residence in designated regional area

additional_flags.title: Understanding of regional living requirement unclear
additional_flags.why_it_matters: The 491 is a provisional visa — to become eligible for permanent residency (Subclass 191), holders must live and work in a designated regional area for at least 3 years. Not understanding this requirement can lead to unmet conditions and consequences for future visa options.
actions: Confirm the intended regional area is on the designated list
Plan employment and accommodation in a qualifying regional area
Understand the pathway from 491 to 191 Permanent Residence
evidence_examples: Job offer in designated regional area
Lease or accommodation in designated regional area

additional_flags.title: Points claimed may exceed what evidence supports
additional_flags.why_it_matters: The 491 uses the same points test as 189/190. Nomination or sponsorship provides 15 bonus points, but all other claimed points must still be evidenced.
actions: Recalculate points including the 15-point regional nomination/sponsorship bonus
Verify each point claim with supporting documents
